titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Lewis and Lois Gilbert Papers>
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Martha Lund Smalley

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday June 5, 2020 at 22:01

did.abstract: Letters and photographs provide documentation of religious and political conditions in North China from 1929 to 1941 and document Lewis Gilbert's work at Yali 1925-1926. Lewis and Lois Gilbert were missionaries in North China from 1929 to 1941 serving under the American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions. Lewis Gilbert was also an instructor at Yali, Yale-in-China, Changsha from 1925 to 1926.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Lewis and Lois Gilbert Papers (RG 144), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

chronitemset.event: Lewis Loder Gilbert born in New Haven, CT
Went to China as instructor in Religion at Yali, Yale-in-China, Changsha, Hunan Province
Returned to China, attending language school in Peking, and then serving the ABCFM in Lintsing and Tehchow

Plans to return to China as professor of New Testament at Cheeloo University were abandoned because of health problem. Gilbert was involved in UCC work in Ohio, Vermont and Massachusetts until his retirement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Gilbert papers provide valuable documentation of religious and political conditions in North China from 1929 to 1941, and document Lewis' work at Yali, 1925-1926. The original handwritten or typed letters of Series I are primarily from Lewis and Lois Gilbert in China to their families in the U.S. The bound letter copybooks of Series II contain primarily letters from Lewis Gilbert to family members. A few of the earlier volumes also include listings of headlines from Chinese newspapers. The bound volumes of correspondence of Series III were edited and compiled by Josephine Cowin Gilbert and have photographic illustrations interspersed.

corpname.part: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions -- China

did.unittitle: Primarily from Lewis and Lois Gilbert in China to their families in the U.S
Primarily from Lewis and Lois Gilbert in China to their families in the U.S
Biographical statement re. Lewis Gilbert written by Josephine Gilbert
Printed circular letters of Lewis and Lois Gilbert
Typescript circular letters of Lewis Gilbert written from Hsiachin and Lintsing, Shantung, incl. re. Japanese occupying troops, flood relief efforts
Typescript letters from Lewis Gilbert to American Board office in Boston, incl. re. flood relief efforts in Lintsing area
Typescript letter fragments, probably from Lewis Gilbert, incl. to Earle Ballou
Circular letters and bulletins from other missionaries: Arthur Allen family, Earle Ballou, Albert and Clara Hausske, Lucia Lyons, Alice Murphy, David Paton, Alice Reed
Circular letters and bulletins from other missionaries: Harold W. Robinson (including long letter re. political situation in Tehchow, Shantung area in 1938), Robert Whitaker, unidentified
Circular letters from Yale-in-China missionaries Phillips Greene and William Pettus, one issue of Yali News Bulletin

Correspondence, newsclippings, and commentary re. "Tehchow Incident" in which an American Board missionary doctor Francis F. Tucker shot and killed a thief who was stealing items from the mission hospital
Typescript documents re. Sino-Japanese situation in northern China
Miscellaneous typescript documents, including autobiography of H.C. Li, translated by L.L. Gilbert, and "The Grace of God Comes to the Flood Victims" by Chou Ming Lin, translated by Lucia Lyons

list.item: "To Our Friends from Alice and Willard Simpson, Horticultural Experiment Station, East Cliff, Peitaiho Beach China / How May Chinese Agriculture Be Improved?"

"The Anti-Christian Movement" (A collection of papers originally issued by the Anti-Christian Movement and translated for the Student Young Men's and Young Women's Christian Associations of China, Shanghai, 1925)
"The American Board in Shantung" by Luella Miner
"A Statement to Chinese Friends" by a group of Nanking missionaries (re. Nanking Affair, 1927)
"Constitution of the North China Christian Rural Fellowship"
Report of the Peking Union Bible Training Institute for Women (1941)

did.unittitle: Poster: with translation "If the Communist Devil does not die wild turmoil can never end - To tolerate Communism and oppose Japan is the destruction of China. Awake quickly and establish a happy land."; schematic map of American Board mission in Paotingfu
American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions (ABCFM): China Bulletin
ABCFM: Post-War Japan Bulletin, Alice Cary

ABCFM: Reports and letters, including regarding North China Christian Rural Service Union, "The Mission Mirror", "Report of Visitation in the North" by E.L. Chapple, Auburndale Missionary Conference, North China Kung Li Hui, China Committee
ABCFM, Reports and letters, including to/from Wynn C. Fairfield, report on Lu Ho Middle School, Sian, Shensi
ABCFM: Reports and letters, including from Harold Matthew, re. reestablishment of North China mission after war, North China Christian Rural Service Union
ABCFM: Reports and letters, including from Harold Matthew, re. reestablishment of North China mission after war, relief work in the Te-Lin area
ABCFM: Circular letters of individuals, including Earle Ballou, Grace Boynton, Margaret Dow, Elmer Galt, Albert Hausske, Samuel Leger, Alice Reed, Harold Robinson,
National Christian Council of China (NCCC): radio broadcast transcripts
